name: Aekvator kaffe
comment:
Espresso Equador 250gr. Bought in store in Nyborg. 
I think it was old when I bought it, beans very dry and tasteless. 
I ended up using the beans in my plants to deter gnats. 

Was going to buy online but they only accept dankort or bank transfer, not even mobile pay. I really cannot be bothered.

name: Als Risteri
comment:
Als espresso "full city roast"  220gr  
. Not a bad coffee by nany means. I found that if I set the grinder to a 7 or 8 I got the most flavour without too much acidity.

 Any courser and it turned watery. 
  I definetly like this one the most with regular whole milk or skimmed milk.
 The oatmilk is quite sweet and found it overpowered the mild flavour. Super quick service, they shipped the same day I rderer and I got it to my pakkeshop the day after

name: Andersen & Maillard
comment: Lalesa Black Honey 250gr. This one was a delight. I broke my always purchase coffee from a new roaster rule just to get another bag of this. The coffee made me think of cherries, and as weird as that sounds, I cannot get enough of it. I kept the grinder a little courser here because the beans were very glossy (oily?) and a finer setting just created over extracted tar. I loved everything about this coffee and found it perfect with the sweet thick oat milk. Loved this. Excellent quick service. Not the cheapest but 100% worth it.

name: April Coffee Roasters
comment: I did not venture into trying the 600kr for 125gr. But I went for a 250gr decaf and a 250gr House blend keneya. Both terrific. It is not too easy to find good decaf and this made for a very full coffee without the slightly watery or even toxic taste that I often get from decaf. This one did very well with the fatter whole milk and the Oatly. I personally thought the skimmed milk did not do it justice. The Kenya was ok. Not a bad coffee not a fantastic one either. I kept it to a grind setting 8 usually after trying even down to 6. Stellar fast service.

name: Buchwalds
comment:
Buchwald's Espresso - 250g, I am dying to visit Bornholm one day, though it was especially interesting to try oat a roastery from this more distant f the danish Islands.
 Somewhat mild espresso that I mostly used in skimmed milk and with a finer grinding setting. The Oatmilk hid the coffee falvour instead if bringing it out. This one was a little tricky for me. I used a much finer setting than I normally do for any espresso to get the most flavour out. But once I got there I really enjoyed it.
 Loved the design.
 Flawless checkout and payment service altough GLS decided to hold on to this for an extra couple of days so it took a little longer than most to get the coffee.

name: Clever Coffee
comment: Kindeng - Papua Ny Guinea, 250gr. Insanely good. This was a clear favourite from the start. It was hard to screw this one up. I loved it with Oatly, whole and skimmed milk and actually just found various grinding settings to bring out interest more than possible ruin the coffee. Stellar service. Had a brewed cup the day after I purchased online. Lovely coffee.

comment: Subscription have gotten I think 10 different kinds

name: Just Coffee
comment:
Espresso Slowdown koffeinfri  250gr 92kr +shipment 35kr . Really good decaf. Ended up buying an extra kilo.
Note, I love their Ethiopian Natural and put trying new roasteries on hold for a while just to have that coffee for a month.

name: Strandvejsristeriet
comment:
Espresso Velo
Espressoblend med Guatemala, Huehuetenango, og vores brasilianske arabica kaffe fra Fazenda Rainha, som er af sorten yellow bourbon, og en pulped natural kaffe med en stor naturlig sødme, når den er ristet korrekt.
Hertil en portion Etiopien, Sidamo som ikke er vasket, men natural processeret på farmen Nefas og giver elegant sødme og tyngde til blandingen. For at give frugt og fylde er der til slut iblandet lidt økologisk Honduras fra Capucas.

name: Svendborg Kafferisteri
comment:
Decaf Arbica 250g 
Bought in store , the coffeee was pretty stale and old. 
Can't reallly compete with coffeees directly from the roasteries that are fresh.

name: Te & Kaffe specialisten
comment:
Kaffe med julet smag 225g 
Bought in store. Not good for espresso even if store attendant assured it was fine for espresso. Got a watery cinnamon tasting sewer water out of this. 
Perfectly fine for french press. 
Its not possible to purchase over the website. Need to find in a store somewhere. Not sure I can be bothered

name: Winthers Kaffe
comment: Can´t see on the website where the roastery is. The shop is in Odense and they claim their coffeee is freshly roasted.
